text:
Thank you, Brother Bosworth, and good evening, friends. It's a pleasure to be back here again tonight to serve the Lord, and I'm very happy to see you people gathered out in this time of worship.
I just shook hands with an old friend of mine from down in Miami. Reminds me … seems like I wouldn't feel like coming to Florida, if I didn't get to see what we call, Brother Jim. So I'm very happy to have him here with us tonight on the platform. Much water has passed down the river since I seen you last, Brother Jim. Been many places around the world. Going again, if God willing, in the near future.
We're very happy that God did what He did for the people this afternoon, blessing them, and I trust now that right quick … I've been some last two hours or more, constantly praying and I just want to testify a little tonight, and then go right into the service.
text_no:
Takk, Bror Bosworth, og god kveld, venner. Det er en glede å være tilbake her igjen i kveld for å tjene Herren, og jeg er veldig glad for å se dere samlet til denne tid av tilbedelse.
Jeg hilste nettopp på en gammel venn fra Miami. Det minner meg om at det virker som om jeg ikke ville følt det samme om jeg kom til Florida uten å se det vi kaller Bror Jim. Så jeg er veldig glad for å ha ham her med oss i kveld på plattformen. Mye vann har rent ned elven siden sist jeg så deg, Bror Jim. Jeg har vært mange steder rundt om i verden og skal reise igjen, om Gud vil, i nær fremtid.
Vi er veldig glade for at Gud velsignet folket i ettermiddag, og jeg stoler på at vi raskt ... Jeg har de siste to timene eller mer vært i konstant bønn, og jeg vil bare vitne litt i kveld før vi går direkte inn i møtet.

text:
First I shall read some of His Word and knowing that His Word is perfect and will never fail. It's found in the 4th chapter of Saint John beginning with the 46th verse.
… Jesus came again into Cana, of Galilee, where he made the water wine. And there was a certain nobleman, whose son was sick at Capernaum.
And when he heard that Jesus was coming out of Judea into Galilee, he went unto him, and besought him that he would come down, and heal his son; for he was at the point of death.
Then said Jesus unto him, Except ye see signs and wonders, ye will not believe.
The nobleman said unto him, Sir, come … ere my child die.
Jesus said unto him, Go thy way; thy son liveth. And the man believed the word that Jesus had spoken unto him, and he went his way.
And as he was now going down, his servant came to him, and told him, saying, Thy son liveth.
And he enquired of them the hour when he began to amend. And they said unto him, Yesterday at the seventh hour the fever left him.
So the father knew that it was the same hour, which Jesus said unto him, Thy son liveth; and … himself believed, and all of his household.
This is again the second miracle which Jesus did, when he … came out of Judea into Galilee.
text_no:
Først skal jeg lese noe av Hans Ord og vite at Hans Ord er perfekt og aldri vil feile. Det er hentet fra det fjerde kapittelet i Evangeliet etter Johannes, med start i det 46. verset.
Jesus kom igjen til Kana i Galilea, hvor Han gjorde vann til vin. Der var det en viss adelsmann hvis sønn var syk i Kapernaum.
Da han hørte at Jesus kom fra Judea til Galilea, gikk han til Ham og bønnfalte Ham om å komme ned og helbrede sønnen hans; for han var nær døden.
Da sa Jesus til ham: "Hvis dere ikke ser tegn og under, vil dere ikke tro."
Adelsmannen sa til Ham: "Herre, kom før barnet mitt dør."
Jesus sa til ham: "Gå, din vei; sønnen din lever." Og mannen trodde på det Ord Jesus hadde talt til ham, og han gikk sin vei.
Mens han var på vei ned, kom tjeneren hans og møtte ham og sa: "Din sønn lever."
Da spurte han dem om det tidspunktet da han begynte å bli bedre. Og de sa til ham: "I går ved den syvende time forlot feberen ham."
Da visste faren at det var samme time som Jesus hadde sagt til ham: "Din sønn lever," og han selv trodde, sammen med hele huset hans.
Dette var igjen det andre miraklet Jesus gjorde, da Han kom fra Judea til Galilea.

text:
Can we bow our heads now while we speak to Him just a moment in prayer.
Our Father who art in heaven, hallowed be Thy name. We will worship Thee, tonight our Father in the name of Thy beloved Son, Jesus Christ, and we love Thee because that Thou hast first loved us, and redeemed us from a life of sin, and has given us a heart of worship. And we will praise Thee and receive tonight, O God, the adoration of our hearts as we pour it out in simplicity to Thee, loving Thee, and knowing this that Thou alone holds our lives, and some day we must meet Thee face to face and give an account for our life here on earth.
What type of people should we be then, Father? We can do nothing except Thou will help us. And tonight while this little group of people gathered together here on the evening of the Sabbath, we thank Thee for this lovely day and for the many sermons, and the ministers across the world who has preached the Gospel, all the souls that has believed on Thee, the great miracles and wonders, no doubt around the world being performed right now. And dear Saviour, don't pass us tonight.
text_no:
Kan vi bøye våre hoder nå mens vi taler til Ham et øyeblikk i bønn.
Vår Far i himmelen, helliget vorde Ditt navn. Vi tilber Deg i kveld, vår Far, i Din elskede Sønn, Jesus Kristus, sitt navn. Vi elsker Deg fordi Du først elsket oss og forløste oss fra et liv i synd. Du har gitt oss et hjerte for tilbedelse, og vi vil prise Deg. Mottak i kveld, Gud, våre hjerters tilbedelse når vi hellig med enkelhet skjenker den til Deg, idet vi elsker Deg og vet at Du alene holder våre liv. En dag må vi møte Deg ansikt til ansikt og gjøre regnskap for våre liv her på jorden.
Hvilke slags mennesker bør vi være da, Far? Vi kan ingenting gjøre uten Din hjelp. Og i kveld, mens denne lille gruppen mennesker har samlet seg her på sabbatskvelden, takker vi Deg for denne vakre dagen, for de mange prekenene og predikantene verden over som har forkynt Evangeliet, og for alle sjelene som har trodd på Deg. Takk for de store miraklene og undrene som uten tvil utføres rundt om i verden i dette øyeblikk. Kjære Frelser, gå ikke forbi oss i kveld.

text:
We pray that You will come in Your magnificent power and will lay Thy healing wings across this building, and may every sick person in here be filled with healing power. Grant it, Lord. May, as the prophet said, the distilled dewdrops of mercy bathe our souls until we see the purpose of Jesus, our King, to redeem us from sickness and sin and to set us free and to give us a happy life of service winning others for Him. These blessings we ask Thee to grant to us, unworthy ones, in the name of Thy Son, Jesus. Amen.
Just a testimony tonight. Taken so much of your time this afternoon in preaching. My son told me, said, "Dad, you didn't realize you was about an hour and forty-five minutes."
text_no:
Vi ber om at Du vil komme i Din majestetiske kraft og legge Dine helbredende vinger over denne bygningen, slik at hver syk person her inne fylles med helbredende kraft. Gi det, Herre. Måtte, som profeten sa, de destillerte duggdroper av nåde bade våre sjeler til vi ser Jesu, vår Konges, hensikt med å fri oss fra sykdom og synd, og med å sette oss fri for et lykkelig liv i tjeneste for å vinne andre for Ham. Disse velsignelsene ber vi Deg gi til oss uverdige, i Din Sønns, Jesu, navn. Amen.
Bare et vitnesbyrd i kveld. Jeg tok så mye av tiden deres i ettermiddag med forkynnelse. Min sønn sa til meg: "Pappa, du var ikke klar over at du holdt på i omtrent en time og førtifem minutter."

text:
Well, I just love to talk about Him so much that I just can't get through talking. I've been twenty-three years now in the ministry and I come in the ministry when I was just a boy and the only regrets that I have if I could spend my life of one thing that I regret more than anything else that I didn't know Jesus when I was just a little bitty boy. I wished I could turn it back. Every hour of my life I would give for His glory.
I remember as a young boy just twenty… Why, the boys used to say, "Billy, you don't get any pleasure out of life. You don't dance. You don't drink. You don't smoke. You don't go to shows or dances. What do you get out of life?"
And one night a young lady asked me that when and I was taking her home. She said, "What do you get out of life?"
text_no:
Jeg elsker å snakke om Ham så mye at jeg aldri blir ferdig. Jeg har vært i tjenesten i tjue-tre år nå. Jeg kom inn i tjenesten som ung gutt, og mitt eneste anger er at jeg ikke kjente Jesus da jeg var en liten gutt. Jeg skulle ønske jeg kunne spole tiden tilbake. Hver time av mitt liv ville jeg ha gitt for Hans ære.
Jeg husker, som en ung gutt på bare tjue år, at guttene pleide å si: "Billy, du får ingen glede ut av livet. Du danser ikke, drikker ikke, røyker ikke, og du går ikke på kino eller dans. Hva får du ut av livet?"
En kveld spurte en ung dame meg det samme da jeg fulgte henne hjem. Hun sa: "Hva får du ut av livet?"

text:
And the next night I made an altar call in the place where I was preaching, a local Baptist church and down the aisle come thirty or forty people kneeling at the altar. The young lady was sitting there weeping. I said, "Would you stand up, sister?" I said, "You wanted to know where I got my pleasures. This is worth more to me than all the things that the world ever give me--this one thing here."
It gives such a peace and a satisfaction to my soul. I'm so happy tonight that Almighty God in His infinite mercy, and His redeeming grace saw fit one time to save me, and to give me the privilege of speaking to His people. I love people. I love everyone, the world, whether they're sinners or Christians. I feel that in my heart.
text_no:
Neste kveld holdt jeg en alterkall i den lokale baptistmenigheten hvor jeg forkynte. Tretti til førti mennesker kom ned midtgangen og knelte ved alteret. Den unge kvinnen satt der og gråt. Jeg spurte: "Vil du reise deg opp, søster?" Jeg sa: "Du ville vite hvor jeg finner min glede. Dette her er verdt mer for meg enn alt verden noensinne kan gi meg."
Det gir en slik fred og tilfredsstillelse til min sjel. Jeg er så takknemlig i kveld for at Den Allmektige Gud i Sin uendelige nåde og forløsende miskunn en gang så det passende å frelse meg, og gi meg privilegiet å tale til Hans folk. Jeg elsker mennesker. Jeg elsker alle, enten de er syndere eller kristne. Dette føler jeg i mitt hjerte.

text:
Every one of us … Jesus died for all of us. I see the men sometime out on the street drinking and I see them gambling but… And some of them cursing, but that doesn't turn me against the person. It just only makes me know that Satan's doing that. That's my brother and it's such… It just hurts me to see Satan having grip like that. Now if I can say something to him to win him over to our Lord Jesus so he can have eternal life, why he will stop that. He is my brother. He's just fallen, and I love to help him up, see. And that's truly from my heart.
And if I should die tonight, the testimony that I have given which has now went around the world. And if I should die tonight, and this would be my last sermon that I'd ever speak, or last prayer line I'd ever pray for the sick, my testimony is truth. And if I'd meet you at the judgment, you'll find out that I have only testified what is the truth of Jesus Christ and His mercy.
text_no:
Hver eneste en av oss ... Jesus døde for oss alle. Jeg ser menn på gaten som drikker og spiller, og noen av dem banner. Men det får meg ikke til å vende meg mot personen. Det gjør meg bare oppmerksom på at det er Satan som påvirker dem. Det er min bror, og det gjør vondt å se at Satan har et slikt grep om dem. Hvis jeg kan si noe for å vinne ham over til vår Herre Jesus, slik at han kan få evig liv, vil han slutte med det. Han er min bror. Han har bare falt, og jeg elsker å hjelpe ham opp. Og det kommer virkelig fra hjertet.
Hvis jeg skulle dø i natt, ville vitnesbyrdet jeg har gitt, som nå har nådd rundt hele verden, stå fast. Hvis jeg skulle dø i natt, og dette var min siste preken eller siste bønnekø for de syke, så er mitt vitnesbyrd sant. Om jeg møter deg ved dommen, vil du oppdage at jeg bare har vitnet om sannheten om Jesus Kristus og Hans nåde.

text:
Sometime ago, just in this little testimony, I was at Houston, Texas, and I was having a meeting. We were having somewhat, about eight thousand in a coliseum, and Mr. Bosworth and I was together, my brother here. We were having a lovely meeting. Raymond Richey and many of the churches around was sponsoring the meeting and there came a man…
I have plenty of critics. We know that. We're bound too. If Jesus had them, well, I've got to have them too and so have you. We all have them, but there was a man just out of a seminary, and he was a very smart, brilliant man. He was a doctor, had just got his degree and he was a Baptist minister. His name was Doctor Best, and he was a very brilliant young man. He had a nice tabernacle there, and a very fine following, and some of his people was coming over to the meeting, and so he took the side to say that there was no such a thing as divine healing.
Well, Mr. Bosworth, in fixing the itinerary and the advertisement and Mr. Richey and many of the other ministers had hung a sign out in the street said, "Miracles every night."
text_no:
For en tid tilbake holdt jeg et møte i Houston, Texas. Vi var rundt åtte tusen mennesker i en koliseum, sammen med Bror Bosworth og meg. Min bror her var også til stede. Vi hadde en flott samling. Raymond Richey og flere menigheter i området støttet møtet. Da dukket det opp en mann...
Jeg har mange kritikere. Vi vet det. Vi må ha dem. Hvis Jesus hadde dem, så må også jeg og du ha dem. Vi har alle våre kritikere. Men denne mannen var nettopp uteksaminert fra et seminar, en svært smart og briljant mann. Han var doktor og hadde nylig fått sin grad. Han var også en baptistpredikant, ved navn Doktor Best. Han var en svært intelligent ung mann som hadde en flott tabernakel og stor tilhengerskare. Noen av hans følgere kom til møtet vårt, noe som fikk ham til å påstå at det ikke eksisterte noe som heter guddommelig helbredelse.
Vel, da Bror Bosworth planla reiseruten og annonseringen sammen med Mr. Richey og flere andre predikanter, hadde de hengt opp et skilt på gaten hvor det stod, "Mirakler hver kveld."

text:
Well, Mr. Best said, "Well, that was ridiculous and there was no such a thing as divine healing," and he put a challenge in the paper instead of coming to us. He put a challenge in the paper and said, "William Branham is a religious impostor and," said, "he should be run out of the city, and I should be the man to do it."
So I didn't think it sounded very Christian in my brother but that was all right and Mr. Bosworth came and he said, "Brother Branham, look at this." And he challenged me to debate the subject of divine healing with him openly before the people. Well, I'm not a debater. I don't believe in fussing. I preach the Gospel. If the people believe it, why, all right. If they don't, it's all I can do about it.
text_no:
Mr. Best sa: "Det er absurd, og det finnes ikke noe slikt som guddommelig helbredelse." I stedet for å komme til oss, utfordret han oss i avisen og sa: "William Branham er en religiøs svindler og bør drives ut av byen. Jeg burde være mannen som gjør det."
Jeg syntes ikke det hørtes spesielt kristent ut av ham, men det var greit. Mr. Bosworth kom til meg og sa: "Bror Branham, se på dette." Han utfordret meg til åpen debatt om emnet guddommelig helbredelse foran folk. Vel, jeg er ikke en debattant. Jeg tror ikke på å krangle. Jeg forkynner Evangeliet. Hvis folk tror på det, fint. Hvis ikke, er det lite jeg kan gjøre med det.

text:
And you never make anything by fussing and debating. Don't never do it because you just make enemies. That's all. I've seen so much of it. So Mr. Bosworth, he came and he said, "Brother Branham, look at this."
I said, "Brother Bosworth, just let it alone." I said, "We got thousands of people to be prayed for, and why would we fool with one person who doesn't believe when there's thousands waiting to be prayed for who does believe?" I said, "Why fool with it?"
Well, the next day the minister put in the paper a great headline in Houston Chronicle and he said, "It goes to show what they're made out of. They're ashamed and afraid when the real Gospel is laid out before them, to debate the subject." Says, "It shows what they're made out of."
Well, here come Brother Bosworth to my room again. He said, "Looky here, Brother Branham. Are you going to take that?"
text_no:
Du oppnår ingenting ved å krangle og diskutere. Gjør det aldri fordi du bare skaper fiender. Det er alt. Jeg har sett så mye av det. Så kom Mr. Bosworth og sa, "Bror Branham, se på dette."
Jeg svarte, "Bror Bosworth, bare la det være." Jeg sa, "Vi har tusenvis av mennesker som skal bli bedt for, så hvorfor bruke tid på én person som ikke tror, når det er tusenvis som venter på å bli bedt for og som tror?" Jeg sa, "Hvorfor bry seg med det?"
Neste dag skrev denne predikanten en stor overskrift i Houston Chronicle. Han sa, "Dette viser hva de virkelig er laget av. De er skamfulle og redde når det virkelige Evangeliet blir lagt frem for dem, og unngår å diskutere emnet." Han sa, "Dette viser hva de er laget av."
Vel, her kom Bror Bosworth til rommet mitt igjen. Han sa, "Se her, Bror Branham. Skal du virkelig godta dette?"

text:
And I said, "Why, Brother Bosworth, it won't do any good." I said, "The Holy Spirit is with me. He promised me He would do, and I have the anointing to pray for sick people and I just won't fool with it."
He said, "Let me do it." And said, "Let me… It's a shame." Said, "The public will think that we're just a bunch of people that's just … just flying about, that there's no Scripture to it or nothing." Said, "We owe it to the public."
I said, "I only owe the public what God told me to do."
And he said, "Well, Brother Branham," he said, "it's not right." Said, "Them men hasn't got…" Said, "I can produce hundreds of Scriptures and they haven't even got … that man hasn't got one Scripture in the Bible to stand on," and said, "let me prove it."
text_no:
Jeg sa: "Bror Bosworth, det vil ikke gjøre noen nytte." Jeg fortsatte: "Den Hellige Ånd er med meg. Han lovet at Han ville være det, og jeg har salvelsen til å be for syke mennesker, så jeg vil ikke tukle med dette."
Han svarte: "La meg gjøre det." Han la til: "Det er en skam. Offentligheten vil tro at vi bare er en gjeng folk som svever rundt uten Skriftgrunnlag eller noe som helst. Vi skylder offentligheten dette."
Jeg sa: "Jeg skylder offentligheten bare det Gud har bedt meg om å gjøre."
Han sa: "Men, Bror Branham, det er ikke riktig. De mennene har ikke…" Han fortsatte: "Jeg kan fremlegge hundrevis av skriftsteder, og de har ikke engang ett skriftsted i Bibelen å stå på. La meg bevise det."

text:
Well, I thought of Caleb, you know, when that old fellow that had the sword and said, "Joshua put this in my hand when I was forty years old and," said, "I'm eighty today. I'm just as good a man today as I was when Joshua give me the sword." Well, I thought that was pretty gallant of him being in his seventies, and Mr. Best in his thirties. I thought just fresh out of the seminary.
I looked at him and I said, "Brother Bosworth, I don't want you to go down there and fuss with that man."
He said, "Brother Branham, I wouldn't fuss with nobody." He said, "I wouldn't do it, but I know one thing I know what the Word of God teaches." He said, "I think you owe it to say something about it."
I said, "All right. You go do it then, but you promise me you won't fuss."
He said, "I promise."
text_no:
Jeg tenkte på Kaleb, du vet, da den gamle mannen med sverdet sa, "Josva ga meg dette da jeg var førti år, og i dag er jeg åtti. Jeg er like sterk i dag som da Josva ga meg sverdet." Jeg syntes det var ganske modig av ham å si dette i syttiårene, mens Mr. Best var i trettiårene, nettopp uteksaminert fra seminaret.
Jeg så på ham og sa, "Bror Bosworth, jeg vil ikke at du skal gå ned og krangle med den mannen."
Han svarte, "Bror Branham, jeg ville aldri krangle med noen. Men én ting vet jeg, og det er hva Guds Ord lærer. Jeg synes du skylder å si noe om det."
Jeg svarte, "Greit. Da kan du gjøre det, men lov meg at du ikke vil krangle."
Han sa, "Jeg lover."

text:
Well, like a little child with Christmas anticipations down the steps he went. The reporters were waiting. They wouldn't let them come to the room up there. So I said, "I wouldn't fuss with him."
So Brother Bosworth, he went down and then, of course, you know how the newspaper can write. Great big headlines "Ecclesiastical Fur Will Fly."--and they really decorated it up real good. So the next day… But here's what made me know that someday, the great church of God is coming together just as sure… You're going to forget your differences.
When communism goes to forcing you right hard, don't worry. You'll come together. God has a way of doing things, see. And so when the great press come on, it was strange. We couldn't stay in that coliseum anymore, we couldn't stay in that place we were at. We had to go over and rent the big Sam Houston Coliseum, seats around thirty thousand.
text_no:
Som et barn med juleforventninger, gikk han ned trappen. Journalistene ventet, men de fikk ikke komme opp til rommet der oppe. Så jeg sa: "Jeg vil ikke krangle med ham."
Bror Bosworth gikk ned, og som dere vet, kan avisen skrive store overskrifter som "Kirkelig Strid Vil Bryte Ut."—og de pyntet virkelig godt på det. Men neste dag... Dette gjorde at jeg visste at en dag vil Guds store menighet komme sammen, helt sikkert... Dere vil glemme forskjellene.
Når kommunismen presser dere hardt, ikke bekymre dere. Dere vil komme sammen. Gud har en måte å gjøre ting på, ser dere. Da pressen kom i stort antall, var det merkelig. Vi kunne ikke bli i det kolosseumet lenger, vi måtte leie det store Sam Houston Coliseum, som har plass til rundt tretti tusen.

text:
And that day they come in by special plane, special trains until that place packed completely full of people. They didn't care whether they was Methodist, Baptist, whether they were Pentecostal Oneness, Twoness, Threeness or church of God, or whether it was riding a one hump camel, two-hump camel, or whatever it was. There was one thing in common, and that was divine healing was at stake, and every man believed in it and here he come to take the part of it. That's what it takes.
Wait till Christianity comes to that spot, and you'll find all the Methodists, and Baptists, and all of them looking just alike then. Everybody coming right together, pressing into the kingdom of God.
text_no:
Den dagen kom de med spesialfly og spesialtog, og fylte plassen helt til randen med folk. De brydde seg ikke om de var metodister, baptister, pinsevenner med én, to eller tre personer i treenigheten, eller fra menigheten til Gud. Uansett om man red en enhumps kamel, tohumps kamel, eller hva det måtte være, var det én fellesnevner: helbredelse var i fokus, og alle trodde på det og ville delta i det. Det er hva som trengs.
Vent til kristne samles på den måten, da vil du se metteodister, baptister og alle andre se like ut. Alle kommer sammen, og presser seg inn i Guds rike.

text:
That settled it with me that day. I looked back there and there was thousands of all different descriptions of people sitting there from all kinds of churches, Catholic and all, everybody placed together.
And so I told Brother Bosworth I wouldn't go down that night". I said, "No, I don't want to hear it." And my brother was with me, and my wife, and little girl.
He said, "Well, you just stay up there at the hotel." I was staying at that time at the Rice Hotel. So I went to the… When it got time to go, I just couldn't hardly stay away. So I got in the cab, and a couple of police come and so they taken me down. I pulled my overcoat up like this, and we went way up in balcony thirty. And I got a seat up there just before all the crowds got packed in.
text_no: Den dagen var avgjørende for meg. Jeg så tilbake og der satt tusenvis av mennesker av alle slags beskrivelser, fra forskjellige menigheter, inkludert katolske, alle samlet. Jeg sa til Bror Bosworth at jeg ikke ville gå ned den kvelden. Jeg sa: "Nei, jeg vil ikke høre det." Min bror, min kone og vår lille jente var med meg. Han svarte: "Vel, bli bare oppe på hotellet." Jeg bodde på Rice Hotel på den tiden. Da tiden kom for å dra, klarte jeg nesten ikke holde meg unna. Så jeg tok en drosje, og et par politimenn fulgte med og tok meg ned til stedet. Jeg trakk opp frakken på denne måten, og vi gikk opp til balkong tretti. Jeg fant et sete der oppe like før folkemengden fylte opp.

text: I was sitting there with my coat up, my wife and boy sitting there. Well, when he come up for the discussion, the moderators and so forth come to the platform. Mr. Cy Ramsar, who used to be Paul Rader's song leader was leading songs for us. And he was singing. Very much of a diplomat on the platform, he was leading the singing, and just before he turned the service over to the moderator he said, "I was reading in the paper the other day where that someone said that William Branham ought to be run out of the city, a religious impostor. Said, "If you people of Houston would put more time running bootleggers out of the city than you would do religious men like that," said, "your city would be better off." And a great roar went up all through the place.
text_no: Jeg satt der med frakken på, sammen med min kone og sønn. Da han kom opp for diskusjonen, kom moderatorene og andre til plattformen. Mr. Cy Ramsar, som pleide å være Paul Rader's forsanger, ledet sangene for oss. Han sang og var en dyktig diplomat på plattformen. Rett før han overlot møtet til moderator, sa han: "Jeg leste i avisen her om dagen at noen mente William Branham burde bli jaget ut av byen, en religiøs bedrager. Hvis folk i Houston brukte mer tid på å få bootleggere ut av byen enn å forfølge religiøse menn som ham, ville byen hatt det bedre." En voldsom applaus brøt ut over hele stedet.

text:
I thought, "Well." And I looked at Brother Bosworth … I'm not saying this because he is sitting behind me and I believe his wife, and son, and daughter-in-law and them, sitting present somewhere here tonight. I'm not saying this… But I admired him, and he walked out. And Mr. Best when he come over, he went and hired photographers to come take his picture and he wanted to take his picture.
He said, "I'm going to skin that old man and take the hide off of him, and tack it on my study door for a memorial to divine healing." Quite a statement for a child of God to say, you know but, he said that. And so he wanted to take the picture.
So they hired the Douglas Studios in Houston, Texas, to come take his picture. And so Mr. Bosworth who had never been in a debate before and so he wanted Mr. Bosworth… Mr. Bosworth felt that he would just stand and give Scriptures and discuss them. So Mr. Bosworth had a great load of Scriptures wrote out. He said "Mr. Best, if…"
text_no:
Jeg tenkte: "Vel." Og jeg så på Bror Bosworth… Jeg sier ikke dette fordi han sitter bak meg, og jeg tror hans kone, sønn og svigerdatter er til stede her i kveld. Jeg sier ikke dette bare av den grunn... Men jeg beundret ham, og han gikk frem. Da Mr. Best kom, hadde han leid fotografer for å ta bilde av ham. Han sa: "Jeg skal flå den gamle mannen og feste huden på døren til arbeidsrommet mitt som et minnesmerke for guddommelig helbredelse." Ganske drastisk utsagn for et Guds barn, vet du, men han sa det. Og derfor ønsket han å ta et bilde.
De leide Douglas Studios i Houston, Texas, for å ta bildet hans. Bror Bosworth, som aldri hadde vært i en debatt før, følte at han bare skulle stå frem, gi Skriftsteder og diskutere dem. Han hadde en stor mengde Skriftsteder skrevet ned. Han sa: "Mr. Best, hvis…"

text:
And Mr. Best said, "No, you go up and say what you're going to say in thirty minutes. Then I'll say what I've got to say in thirty minutes."
So well, they agreed on that. Then had a [puddle like] at the last. Well, I was sitting listening wondering what was going to take place. Well, when I seen my Brother Bosworth walk out there coolly, knowed just where he was at, there's no doubt in his mind.
He said, "Dr. Best," he said, "we're sorry that we have to have discussions like this, but" said "you had a statement in the paper that Brother Branham was a religious impostor and there was no such a thing as divine healing. Said, "Now our subject tonight to discuss is not Brother Branham. It's whether divine healing is in the Bible or not. That's what the debate is for. That's what we signed up for--whether divine healing was taught in the Scriptures."
text_no:
Herr Best sa: "Nei, du går opp og sier det du skal si på tretti minutter. Deretter sier jeg det jeg må si på tretti minutter."
Slik ble de enige om det. Til slutt hadde de et oppgjør. Jeg satt og lyttet, undrende på hva som skulle skje. Da jeg så Bror Bosworth gå rolig ut der, visste jeg at han var trygg på hva han gjorde, uten noen tvil i sitt sinn.
Han sa: "Dr. Best, vi beklager at vi må ha diskusjoner som dette, men du uttalte i avisen at Bror Branham var en religiøs bedrager og at det ikke finnes noe som heter guddommelig helbredelse. Nå er ikke emnet for vår diskusjon Bror Branham. Det er hvorvidt guddommelig helbredelse finnes i Bibelen eller ikke. Det er det debatten handler om. Det er det vi har skrevet under på—om guddommelig helbredelse er lært i Skriften."

text: He said, "The gifts of God will prove themselves, but this is what we're teaching whether divine healing is in the Bible or not." He said, "Our … (I forget how many Scriptures there, a hundred or two) he said, "This is Christ present, His attitude, present attitude towards the sick is just the same today as it ever was." So many Scriptures he had. Said, "If you'll take one of those Scriptures and by the Scripture disprove it, then we won't have any discussion. I've lost the debate, and I will sit down. If you can take the Scriptures and prove that Christ's present attitude isn't just exactly the same towards the sick now as it ever was." And he offered him a paper.
text_no: Han sa: "Guds gaver vil bevise seg selv, men dette er hva vi lærer om hvorvidt guddommelig helbredelse er i Bibelen eller ikke." Han sa: "Vår ... (jeg husker ikke hvor mange Skriftsteder, hundre eller to) han sa, "Dette er Kristus til stede, Hans holdning, nåværende holdning overfor de syke er akkurat den samme i dag som den alltid har vært." Han hadde så mange Skriftsteder. Han sa: "Hvis du tar ett av disse Skriftstedene og ved Skriften motbeviser det, da har vi ingen diskusjon. Jeg har tapt debatten og vil sette meg ned. Hvis du kan bruke Skriftstedene og bevise at Kristi nåværende holdning ikke er nøyaktig den samme overfor de syke nå som den alltid var." Og han tilbød ham et papir.

text:
Mr. Best said, "I'll take care of that when I get up there." Said, "You go ahead with what you're going to say." Said, "I'll take care of that.
And Mr. Bosworth said, "Then Mr. Best, I ask you one question, and if you'll answer me yes or no, I will sit down." He said, "Was the redemptive names of Jehovah applied to Jesus, yes or no?" I never thought of that myself. That settled it. That's all.
He said, "I will answer that."
He said, "I'm asking you to answer me now." Said, "We don't have to discuss it, you say yes or no." Course he couldn't, certainly not. If the redemptive names of Jehovah was not applied to Jesus, He was not the Christ or the Son of God. He wasn't Jehovah-jireh, the Lord's provided sacrifice. And if He was the Lord's provided sacrifice, Jehovah-jireh, He was also Jehovah-rapha, the healer, the same yesterday, today, and forever. That settled it, see.
text_no:
Mr. Best sa: "Jeg tar meg av det når jeg kommer opp dit. Fortsett med det du skal si. Jeg tar meg av det."
Mr. Bosworth sa: "Mr. Best, jeg har ett spørsmål, og hvis du svarer ja eller nei, vil jeg sette meg ned." Han spurte: "Er de forløsningsnavnene til Jehova anvendt på Jesus, ja eller nei?" Jeg hadde aldri tenkt på det selv. Det avgjorde saken. Det var alt.
Mr. Best sa: "Jeg vil svare på det."
Bosworth svarte: "Jeg ber deg svare nå. Vi trenger ikke diskutere det, bare svar ja eller nei." Selvsagt kunne han ikke det. Hvis forløsningsnavnene til Jehova ikke var anvendt på Jesus, var Han verken Kristus eller Guds Sønn. Han var ikke Jehova-jireh, Herrens providede offer. Og hvis Han var Herrens providede offer, Jehova-jireh, så var Han også Jehova-rapha, helbrederen, den samme i går, i dag og for evig. Det avgjorde saken, ser du.

text:
So Mr. Best got up and turned around and kind of shook his fist two or three times and set down. And so Mr. Bosworth stood there and just laid off Scripture. And I never heard it like that in my life, just as coolly, and placed everything to a place where Mr. Best didn't even have one place at all to go to in the Scripture. He just was just tied so perfectly till there was no angle to get out of it. He was just tied down.
Mr. Best got up when his time come and Mr. Bosworth with a very, very polite way, said, "Mr. Best, all right my brother," said, "the platform's yours now." And the moderator give him the platform.
text_no:
Så reiste Mr. Best seg, snudde seg rundt, ristet knyttneven to eller tre ganger, og satte seg ned igjen. Mr. Bosworth stod der og la fram Skrifter på en måte jeg aldri hadde hørt før, rolig og nøyaktig, slik at Mr. Best ikke hadde et eneste sted å gå til i Skriftene. Han var så perfekt bundet at det ikke var noen vei ut.
Da det var Mr. Bests tur, reiste han seg. Mr. Bosworth sa høflig: "Mr. Best, vær så god, min bror," og sa, "plattformen er din nå." Moderator ga ham da ordet.

text:
Mr. Best got up there and laughed two or three times and preached a very good Campbellite sermon. I'm a Baptist. I know what Baptist doctrine is. So he preached a Campbellite sermon on the resurrection. He didn't even believe in miracles that Jesus Christ did. Lazarus died again. He preached it all in the resurrection when this mortal puts on immortality. Of course, any Christian believes that. That there'll be a resurrection, but if there's no attributes of the resurrection, if there's no earnest of the resurrection then there is no resurrection.
If there is no divine healing, then there is no resurrection. Now, you couldn't disprove that if you could by the Scripture. I'm arguing the points of Christ, see, because we have the earnest of our salvation, which is the baptism of the Holy Spirit. Is that right? That's the down payment, the earnest which holds it secure in Heaven for us. And then if there is no divine healing to make a cancer ridden body come normal and well again, there is no resurrection of the dead. That's right. We have no earnest of it at all. There's no plan made for it. So that settled it. Mr. Best commenced to going on, and the audience actually laughed at him.
And so he said, "Let me see that divine healer come forth and perform." He got real angry and he said, "Let me see that divine healer come forth and perform."
text_no:
Mr. Best reiste seg, lo et par ganger, og forkynte en god Campbellite-preken. Jeg er baptist og kjenner til baptistdoktrinen. Han forkynte en Campbellite-preken om oppstandelsen. Han trodde ikke engang på miraklene Jesus Kristus utførte. Lazarus døde igjen, sa han, og han la hele vekt på oppstandelsen når det dødelige ikler seg udødelighet. Selvfølgelig tror enhver kristen på oppstandelsen. Men hvis det ikke finnes noen forvarsler om oppstandelsen, hvis det ikke finnes noe bevis på oppstandelsen, så finnes det ingen oppstandelse.
Hvis det ikke er noen guddommelig helbredelse, finnes det ingen oppstandelse. Du kan ikke motbevise det ut fra Skriften. Jeg argumenterer for Kristi poenger, for vi har pantet på vår frelse, som er dåpen i Den Hellige Ånd. Er det ikke riktig? Det er håndpantet, bekreftelsen som holder det trygt i Himmelen for oss. Og hvis det ikke finnes noen guddommelig helbredelse som kan gjøre en kreftfylt kropp frisk igjen, så finnes det ingen oppstandelse av de døde. Det er riktig. Vi har ingen sikkerhet for dette overhodet. Det er ingen plan for det. Så det avgjorde saken. Mr. Best fortsatte, og publikum lo faktisk av ham.
Han sa, "La meg se at den guddommelige helbrederen kommer frem og utfører et mirakel." Han ble virkelig sint og gjentok, "La meg se at den guddommelige helbrederen kommer frem og utfører et mirakel."

text:
Mr. Bosworth said, "The very idea, Mr. Best, you calling Brother Branham a divine healer." He said, "Never, in all of his life, did ever claim to be a divine healer. Very much contrary, he only preaches divine healing through the atonement. Just exactly like what I proved to you. And you've even mentioned one Scripture that I said or come to any of it. You never done nothing but preached on the resurrection of the dead and we all believe that. So there it was. It was up to the audience to make their choice.
He said, "Let me see that divine healer."
He said, "Brother Branham wasn't to be brought in this. The subject is whether divine healing is taught in the Bible and you can't say one word against it, see. Cause if you can, say it now." So there wasn't nothing to be said 'cause there's was no way he could go because it was all covered over with the atonement and everything. So there it was. There wasn't nothing to be said.
He said, "Let me see that divine healer."
text_no:
Mr. Bosworth sa: "Mr. Best, hvordan kan du kalle Bror Branham en guddommelig helbreder?" Han fortsatte: "Aldri i sitt liv har han hevdet å være en guddommelig helbreder. Tvert imot, han forkynner kun guddommelig helbredelse gjennom soning—akkurat som jeg har bevist for deg. Og du har ikke nevnt et eneste Skriftsted jeg har sitert eller forholdt deg til noe av det. Du har bare talt om oppstandelsen fra de døde, og det tror vi alle på. Valget ligger derfor hos publikum."
Han sa: "La meg se den guddommelige helbrederen."
Bosworth svarte: "Bror Branham skulle ikke trekkes inn i dette. Temaet er om guddommelig helbredelse er lært i Bibelen, og du kan ikke si et ord imot det. Hvis du kan det, så si det nå." Det var ikke noe mer å si, for alt var dekket av soningen. Dermed var saken avgjort.
Han gjentok: "La meg se den guddommelige helbrederen."

text:
And Mr. Bosworth said to him said, "Brother Best, do you believe that God saves the soul?"
He said, "Yes."
"You teach that through the atonement?"
Said, "Yes."
Said, "Would you want to be called a divine saviour?" Said, "If Brother Branham, by preaching divine healing, is a divine healer; then if you preach salvation, you're a divine saviour."
Oh, he got really on the air then, and so he begin to rear up and down. He said, "There's nothing in the world decent in a… Decent-thinking people don't believe in such nonsense." He said, "Baptists don't believe in no such a thing."
Said, "Whoa, beg your pardon," Brother Bosworth said. "Brother Branham was rocked in a Baptist cradle." Said, "That's right."
"Oh," said, "he is a backslidden type." Backslidden type. He said, "There's no such…" Said, "Real true honest Baptists don't believe in no such."
text_no:
Mr. Bosworth spurte ham: "Bror Best, tror du at Gud frelser sjelen?"
Han svarte: "Ja."
"Forkynner du det gjennom forsoningen?"
Han svarte: "Ja."
Mr. Bosworth sa: "Ville du bli kalt en guddommelig frelser? Hvis Bror Branham, ved å forkynne guddommelig helbredelse, er en guddommelig helbreder, da er du en guddommelig frelser når du forkynner frelse."
Da ble Bror Best rasende og begynte å heve stemmen. Han sa: "Fornuftige mennesker tror ikke på slikt tull. Baptister tror ikke på noe slikt."
"Unnskyld," svarte Bror Bosworth. "Bror Branham ble oppvokst i en baptistfamilie."
"Åh," sa han, "han er en frafallen type." Han fortsatte: "Ekte, trofaste baptister tror ikke på slikt."

text:
Brother Bosworth said, "Would you excuse me just a moment?" Said, "How many sitting here in this vast audience that belongs to these fine Baptist churches with all these Baptist ministers sitting along here," said, "how many of you in this audience could prove by a doctor's statement that you've been healed by divine healing--the power of God--in Brother Branham's meetings these last few weeks?" And three hundred stood up. Uh-huh. Said, "What about those?"
Mr. Best hit the pulpit with his fist and said, "People can say anything, but that don't make it right."
But said, "Here's the Word teaches it, and there's the testimony of it. Now, what about it?"
And he said, "Let me see that divine healer."
Then Mr. Richey got up. He said, "Let the moderator speak just a moment." He said, "I know everyone of these Baptist ministers." Said, "Is this the attitude of the Southern Baptist Convention towards divine healing?"--thirty thousand people. "Is this the attitude that the Southern Baptist Convention takes toward divine healing?"
text_no:
Bror Bosworth sa: "Kan jeg få unnskylde meg et øyeblikk?" Han sa, "Hvor mange her i dette store publikummet tilhører disse fine baptistmenighetene med alle disse baptistpastorene sittende her?" og fortsatte, "hvor mange av dere kan bevise med en legeerklæring at dere har blitt helbredet ved guddommelig helbredelse—Guds kraft—i Bror Branhams møter de siste ukene?" Tre hundre reiste seg. "Hva med dem?" spurte han.
Mr. Best slo i talerstolen med knyttneven og sa: "Folk kan si hva som helst, men det gjør det ikke sant."
Bror Bosworth svarte: "Her viser Ordet det, og her er vitnesbyrdene om det. Hva sier du til det?"
Mr. Best svarte: "La meg se den guddommelige helbrederen."
Mr. Richey reiste seg og sa: "La moderator få si noen ord." Han fortsatte: "Jeg kjenner hver eneste av disse baptistpastorene. Er dette holdningen til Southern Baptist-konferansen angående guddommelig helbredelse? Tretti tusen mennesker. Er dette holdningen Southern Baptist-konferansen har til guddommelig helbredelse?"

text:
Nobody would answer. He said, "I'm asking you, brethren. Did you all send this man here, or did he come on his own?"
They said, "He come on his own."
"I thought so. Baptists believe in divine healing. Certainly they do." And so he said, "He come on his own." So that settled it with the Baptist, Southern Baptist church. It was Mr. Best, his own self.
So then he kept saying, "If I see that miracle worker. Let me see him come down here… Let me see him hypnotize one of these people and let me see him a year from now and see if he's still hypnotized." And all like that, you know, just going on. So he said, "Come up here. I want you to take my picture."
And he stood there one time, he put his finger under that saintly old man's face Said, "Now take it."--with his finger shaking. They took the picture. And he drawed his fist and shook it under his nose, you know. Said, "Now, now, take it."--and they took his picture like that. So they took six, nine by ten glossies of him like that. So he went back. There sat the Look, Life, Time, Collier, and all those, and The Chronicle, and all those magazines and papers sitting there.
text_no:
Ingen svarte. Han sa, "Jeg spør dere, brødre. Sendte dere denne mannen hit, eller kom han på egen hånd?"
De sa, "Han kom på egen hånd."
"Det tenkte jeg nok. Baptister tror på guddommelig helbredelse. Selvfølgelig gjør de det." Og så sa han, "Han kom på egen hånd." Da var det avklart med den sørlige baptistmenigheten. Dette var Mr. Best selv.
Deretter fortsatte han: "Hvis jeg ser den mirakelgjøreren. La meg se ham komme hit… La meg se ham hypnotisere en av disse menneskene, og la meg se ham om ett år for å se om han fortsatt er hypnotisert." Og slik fortsatte han. Så sa han, "Kom hit. Jeg vil at du skal ta mitt bilde."
Han sto der en gang og satte fingeren under ansiktet på den hellige gamle mannen og sa: "Nå, ta det." -- med fingeren dirrende. De tok bildet. Han knyttet neven og ristet den under hans nese og sa, "Nå, ta det." -- og de tok bilde av ham slik. De tok seks glansbilder på ni ganger ti tommer av ham på denne måten. Så gikk han tilbake. Der satt representanter fra Look, Life, Time, Collier og mange andre magasiner og aviser som The Chronicle og flere andre.

text:
And then Brother Bosworth said, "Well, now the meeting, as far as I'm concerned, is finished." He said, "Mr. Best cannot answer one question against divine healing besides he has miserably quit. Said, "How many think so? Say, 'Amen.'" And just one great scream went over the building.
And then he got real angry, and walked around, and slapped a minister just about half way across that platform. And then they had to separate him. So then he walked back. Said, "Bring me that divine healer."
Brother Bosworth said, "Now, Brother Branham is sitting in the meeting. I know he is here." Because he seen me up there. He said, "Now if he wants to come and dismiss the audience, all right, but" said "under such a fuss as this, of course no, he couldn't pray for the sick," said, "if you want to see healing come, come over tomorrow night. They'll be going along--all along." And said, "I know he is in the meeting."
Well, I was sitting still. My brother was sitting there. He said, "Now, you sit still. You said you would."
I said, "Well, I'm sitting still."
text_no:
Bror Bosworth sa deretter: "Vel, så langt jeg er bekymret, er møtet ferdig." Han fortsatte: "Mr. Best kan ikke svare på ett eneste spørsmål mot guddommelig helbredelse, og han har dessuten gitt seg elendig. Hvor mange er enige? Si 'Amen.'" En stor jubel brøt ut i bygningen.
Mr. Best ble veldig sint, gikk rundt og slo til en predikant omtrent halvveis over plattformen. De måtte skille ham. Så vendte han tilbake og sa: "Bring meg den guddommelige helbrederen."
Bror Bosworth sa: "Nå, Bror Branham sitter i møtet. Jeg vet at han er her." Han hadde sett meg der oppe. "Hvis han vil komme for å avslutte møtet, er det greit, men under slike omstendigheter kan han selvfølgelig ikke be for de syke," la han til. "Hvis dere vil se helbredelse, kom tilbake i morgen kveld. Helbredelsene vil fortsette." Og så sa han igjen: "Jeg vet at han er i møtet."
Jeg satt stille. Min bror satt der også og sa: "Du sitter stille nå. Du sa du ville."
Jeg svarte: "Vel, jeg sitter stille."

text:
So he said, "And you…" And while I was sitting there like that, I heard something going, "Woooo." I knew He was near; I raised up. My brother said, "Sit down."
My wife said, "No." I looked see, and there it was. So, just then everybody was looking around. They didn't know… No one knew I was up there, and there was just thousands of friends there. And I looked back. People just let out one big scream. Poor, dear, loving people. They'd seen so many things done that week.
So several ushers, hundreds of them, held their arms together like that, and made a line. The people trying to touch your clothes to be healed. Brother, this is not something easy. I'm human. I've got children at home and mothers trying to push their little kids through those ushers arms to touch your clothes when you're passing by. God will reward faith as that. He will. Sure.
And I walked down to the platform. I said, "Brother Best."
He said, "As a man I admire you. In your doctrine you're wrong."
I said, "That's mutually felt." And I said, "I admire you, Brother Best, and you got a right for your ideas." I said, "That's why we're fighting over here in Korea, is the boys is to keep this America, every man to his own opinion."
text_no:
Han sa, "Og du…" Mens jeg satt der, hørte jeg noe som sa, "Woooo." Jeg visste Han var nær; jeg reiste meg. Min bror sa, "Sett deg ned."
Min kone sa, "Nei." Jeg så meg rundt, og der var det. Akkurat da så alle seg omkring. Ingen visste jeg var der oppe, og det var tusenvis av venner der. Jeg snudde meg. Folk ga fra seg et stort skrik. Stakkars, kjære, kjærlige mennesker. De hadde sett så mange ting skje den uken.
Flere av ordensvaktene, hundrevis av dem, holdt armene sammen og dannet en linje. Folk prøvde å røre ved klærne mine for å bli helbredet. Bror, dette er ikke lett. Jeg er menneskelig. Jeg har barn hjemme, og mødre prøvde å presse sine små barn gjennom ordensvaktenes armer for å røre ved klærne mine når jeg gikk forbi. Gud vil belønne slik tro. Det vil Han. Selvsagt.
Jeg gikk ned til plattformen. Jeg sa, "Bror Best."
Han sa, "Som menneske beundrer jeg deg. I din lære tar du feil."
Jeg sa, "Det er gjensidig." Og jeg sa, "Jeg beundrer deg, Bror Best, og du har rett til dine meninger." Jeg sa, "Det er derfor vi kjemper i Korea, for å bevare dette Amerika der hver mann har rett til sin egen mening."

text:
I said, "I don't want any of this people, any of Brother Best's people or any of the Baptist people, anyone to feel bad towards Brother Best." I said, "Don't do that. He is our brother, and he just doesn't see it like that. He just believes it his way and he's got a right to believe it his way." I said, "Remember his mother loves him just the same as my mother loves me, and your mother loves you." I said, "He is somebody's boy and he's got a right to his belief. So don't feel hard at him. It's just… Mr. Bosworth just proved by the Word that the doctrine is sound and in the Bible and Mr. Best could not prove it."
And so Mr. Best just turned around and started off the platform. Then he turned back and looked and sat down. And I said, "Now, I have never said that I was a divine healer." I said, "Never." I said, "My books are published in about seventeen different languages and," I said, "and I guess I'm in contact with several million people, but never have I said a divine healer. I don't heal people."
text_no:
Jeg sa: "Jeg vil ikke at noen av disse menneskene, enten det er Bror Bests folk eller noen av baptistene, skal føle seg dårlige overfor Bror Best." Jeg sa: "Ikke gjør det. Han er vår bror, og han ser det bare ikke på samme måte. Han tror på sin egen måte og har rett til det." Jeg sa: "Husk at hans mor elsker ham like mye som min mor elsker meg, og din mor elsker deg." Jeg sa: "Han er noens sønn og har rett til sin tro. Så ikke se hardt på ham. Det er bare… Herr Bosworth beviste nettopp ved Ordet at læren er solid og i Bibelen, og Herr Best kunne ikke motbevise det."
Så snudde Herr Best seg bare rundt og begynte å gå ned fra plattformen. Deretter snudde han seg tilbake, så på meg og satte seg ned. Jeg sa: "Nå, jeg har aldri sagt at jeg var en guddommelig helbreder." Jeg sa: "Aldri." Jeg sa: "Mine bøker er utgitt på rundt sytten forskjellige språk," og jeg sa: "og jeg er vel i kontakt med flere millioner mennesker, men jeg har aldri sagt at jeg er en guddommelig helbreder. Jeg helbreder ikke mennesker."

text:
I said, "The only thing I do is preach divine healing by the cross, and by the Bible." And I said, "Now when I was a little boy," I said, "my mother said a light hung over me when I was a baby. My people were Catholic before me and my mother and father went to no church. They were Irish on both sides, emigrated from Ireland." And so, I said, "We were … and there, they didn't go to church."
"I was born in a little mountain cabin." I said, "When I was just a little bitty boy, it begin to deal with me. Down through life it always spoke and many, many times and thousands of people have seen it. It comes in like a light." I said, "It tells me things. And when He met me, He told me that it was the Spirit of the Lord Jesus Christ that done just exactly the same things that Brother Bosworth and them has said tonight. Jesus didn't claim to do any healing. He said, 'What the Father shows Me I don't do nothing. I can heal nothing but I do just what the Father shows Me by vision, that's what I do.'" And you know that's the truth, isn't it? Every Bible believer scholar knows that's the truth.
text_no:
Jeg sa: "Det eneste jeg gjør er å forkynne guddommelig helbredelse ved korset og gjennom Bibelen." Jeg fortsatte: "Da jeg var liten gutt, sa min mor at et lys hang over meg da jeg var baby. Mine folk var katolske før meg, og mine foreldre gikk ikke til noen menighet. De var irske på begge sider og emigrerte fra Irland. Så vi gikk ikke til noen menighet."
"Jeg ble født i en liten fjellhytte," sa jeg. "Da jeg var en liten gutt, begynte det å virke på meg. Gjennom livet har det alltid talt til meg, mange, mange ganger og tusenvis av mennesker har sett det. Det kommer inn som et lys." Jeg sa: "Det forteller meg ting. Og da Han møtte meg, fortalte Han meg at det var Herrens Jesu Kristi Ånd som gjorde nøyaktig de samme tingene som Bror Bosworth og de andre har sagt i kveld. Jesus hevdet ikke å gjøre noen helbredelse. Han sa: 'Hva Faderen viser Meg, gjør Jeg ikke noe. Jeg kan ikke helbrede noe, men Jeg gjør bare det Faderen viser Meg gjennom visjoner, det er det Jeg gjør.'" Og dere vet at det er sannheten, ikke sant? Alle bibeltroende forskere vet at det er sannheten.

text:
Said, "He walked through the crowd. He perceived their thoughts. He knowed what they was thinking about. He knowed what was wrong with them. He knowed the woman at the well and He talked to her a little bit where her trouble was. He knowed Nathanael's trouble. And He knowed where a fish had a coin in its mouth, just as God would show Him He would do. Now, He didn't claim to be a healer."
"In the resurrection of Him, He is the same yesterday, today, and forever." And I said, "Now if I testify the truth, God will testify of me. If I do not testify of the truth, God will never have nothing to do with a lie. God will always testify truth though."
I said, "I'm not out here just to be seen. I'm out here, not by choice, by a long ways, I wanted to be a… Well, I was a game warden in the state of Indiana, and I loved my job. I was born for the wilderness and wilds. I certainly… It wasn't my choice to preach the Gospel, but woe unto me if I preach it not. It was God's choice. I run from it and tried to get away from it and everything." I said, "It was Him who brought it." And now, I said, "If I tell the truth, God will testify the truth."
text_no:
Han sa: "Han gikk gjennom mengden. Han forstod deres tanker. Han visste hva de tenkte på. Han visste hva som var galt med dem. Han visste hva som plaget kvinnen ved brønnen og snakket med henne litt om det. Han visste om Natanaels bekymringer. Og Han visste hvor en fisk hadde en mynt i munnen, slik Gud viste Ham. Han hevdet aldri å være en helbreder."
"I Hans oppstandelse er Han den samme i går, i dag og for evig." Og jeg sa: "Hvis jeg vitner om sannheten, vil Gud vitne om meg. Hvis jeg ikke vitner om sannheten, vil Gud aldri ha noe med en løgn å gjøre. Gud vitner alltid om sannhet."
Jeg sa: "Jeg er ikke her for å bli sett. Jeg er her, ikke av eget valg, langt ifra; jeg ønsket å være... Vel, jeg var en viltvokter i delstaten Indiana, og jeg elsket jobben min. Jeg ble født for villmarken og naturen. Det var absolutt ikke mitt valg å forkynne Evangeliet, men ve meg hvis jeg ikke forkynner det. Det var Guds valg. Jeg flyktet fra det og prøvde å unngå det på alle måter." Jeg sa, "Det var Han som førte det hit." Og nå, sa jeg, "Hvis jeg forteller sannheten, vil Gud vitne om sannheten."

text:
And I no more than said it, something went, "Whoosh!" Here He come. Sailing right down where I was, and this photographer run out and took a picture, when he seen me turn white in the face. And he took a picture. I said, "God has spoken, and that's all."
Catholic people and all sitting there saw it, looked at it. Many of them begin to scream and fainting. Come right down, a pillar of fire, moved right down to where I was at and shot the picture.
And the men… One of the men of the studio was a Jew. His name was Ted Kipperman. The other one's name was Mr. Iris. He is a Roman Catholic. And oh, he just wrote a awful thing in the paper about me the other day, the day before that. He said, "I was a hypnotizer," and I don't know what all.
And so, on the road home that night … taken the negatives--going in to have them developed for Mr. Best. He said, "I might have been wrong." Said, "What do you think about that, Ted?"
Ted said, "Well, I'm Jew. I don't know nothing about it."
text_no:
Så snart jeg hadde sagt det, skjedde det noe: "Whoosh!" Der kom Han, seilende rett ned til der jeg sto. Fotografen løp ut og tok et bilde da han så at jeg ble blek i ansiktet. Jeg sa, "Gud har talt, og det er alt som betyr noe."
Katolske og andre tilstedeværende så det og stirret. Mange av dem begynte å skrike og besvime. En ildsøyle kom rett ned til der jeg sto og bildet ble tatt.
En av mennene i studioet var jøde ved navn Ted Kipperman. Den andre het Mr. Iris og var romersk katolikk. Han skrev nettopp en stygg artikkel om meg i avisen dagen før. Han påsto at jeg var en hypnotisør og jeg vet ikke hva annet.
På vei hjem den kvelden, med negativene for å få dem fremkalt for Mr. Best, sa han, "Kanskje jeg tok feil." Han spurte, "Hva synes du om det, Ted?"
Ted svarte, "Vel, jeg er jøde. Jeg vet ingenting om det."

text:
He said, "Well," he said, "I'm Catholic and," said, "we're taught that those things can happen but it can only happen in a Catholic church."
Now, you're wrong there, see? It can happen wherever God wants it to happen whether it's in Catholic church or anywhere He wants it to happen. He is God. He does the choice. He keeps the books.
And so he went in, and he put the pictures in the dark room to go through the acids to be developed and the next… After a bit, why, Mr. Kipperman, he said, "I'm going up and lie down a little while." So he went upstairs to lie down.
And while he was up there, Mr. Iris smoked a cigarette or two, went in the dark room and said, "I will see." He had to get the picture ready because Mr. Best--Dr. Best, the minister, wanted his pictures the next day so he could show it to the people--him shaking his fist under his nose.
text_no:
Han sa: "Vel," sa han, "jeg er katolikk, og vi er lært opp til at slike ting kan skje, men at det bare kan skje i en katolsk kirke."
Nei, der tar du feil. Det kan skje hvor som helst Gud ønsker det, enten det er i en katolsk kirke eller hvor som helst annet Han vil det skal skje. Han er Gud. Han bestemmer. Han holder bøkene.
Så han gikk inn og la bildene i mørkerommet for å gjennomgå syrene for å bli fremkalt. Etter en stund sa Mr. Kipperman: "Jeg går opp for å hvile litt." Så han gikk opp for å legge seg.
Mens han var der oppe, røykte Mr. Iris en sigarett eller to, gikk inn i mørkerommet og sa: "Jeg skal ta en titt." Han måtte gjøre bildet klart fordi Dr. Best, forkynneren, ønsket bildene neste dag for å vise dem til folkene—ham som rister neven under nesen hans.

text:
So then when he… The next when Mr. Iris went in, which is an arch enemy of mine and Brother Bosworth too. He didn't believe it. He wrote a piece in the paper and said I wasn't nothing but a hypnotizer. A goiter had left a woman's throat. He said, "He hypnotized that off of her throat." Oh, my! That shows mental weakness.
So then when he pulled the first picture out of Mr. Best with Mr. Bosworth, it was negative. God wouldn't permit that taken against that saintly old man. He pulled out the next one--negative. He pulled out all six of them, and every one of them was blank. And when he pulled out the other one, there was the Angel of God on the picture. He had a heart attack.
They sent for me at the Rice Hotel. Of course, there's two men standing at the door in big meetings where they have thousands and thousands of people. There's someone stands at the door. They wouldn't let them in. All right.
text_no:
Når han så... Neste gang Mr. Iris kom inn, som er en erklært fiende av både meg og Bror Bosworth. Han trodde ikke på det. Han skrev en artikkel i avisen og hevdet at jeg bare var en hypnotisør. En kvinnes struma hadde forsvunnet fra halsen, og han sa: "Han hypnotiserte den bort fra halsen hennes." Å, min! Det viser mental svakhet.
Da han trakk ut det første bildet av Mr. Best sammen med Mr. Bosworth, var det negativt. Gud tillot ikke at det ble tatt imot den hellige, gamle mannen. Han trakk ut det neste bildet – negativt. Han trakk ut alle seks av dem, og hver eneste var blank. Da han trakk ut det siste, var Guds Engel på bildet. Han fikk et hjerteinfarkt.
De sendte bud på meg til Rice Hotel. Selvfølgelig, ved store møter hvor det er tusenvis av mennesker, står det alltid vakter ved dørene. De slapp ikke folk inn. Greit.

text:
And then that night at eleven o'clock, that was sent to Washington, DC to be copyrighted. On the following day it was flown back again. And they called in George J. Lacy. He is the head of the FBI in fingerprint and documents. The best there is in the world, the best in the United States. So being American, we'd say he was the best in the world to us.
And so he is the head of the FBI. And they brought him in, and he took it to the Shell Building under examination of ultra-ray lights and so forth, everything he tried. He went out, he measured the distance, and took the camera to see if it was a double exposure, and he done everything that he could do to find out. And on about the fourth day he said they was going to meet in the Shell Building and give the announcement--what it was. So that day he said, "Whose name is Branham?"
text_no:
Den kvelden, klokken elleve, ble det sendt til Washington, D.C. for opphavsrettsbeskyttelse. Dagen etter ble det fløyet tilbake. De tilkalte George J. Lacy, leder for FBI innen fingeravtrykk og dokumenter. Han regnes som den beste i sitt felt, både i USA og internasjonalt.
De tok ham med til Shell-bygningen for undersøkelse med ultrafiolett lys og andre metoder. Han målte avstanden og sjekket kameraet for å utelukke dobbel eksponering. Han gjorde alt han kunne for å finne ut av det. På den fjerde dagen bestemte de seg for å møtes i Shell-bygningen for å offentliggjøre resultatet. Den dagen spurte han: "Hvem heter Branham?"

text:
I said, "Mine." Bunch sitting there--a lot of the newspapers and things. Wanted to see what his report was.
He said, "Stand up." I stood up. He said, "You're going to pass out of this life someday like all mortals, Reverend Branham."
I said, "I'm aware of that, but thank God," I said, "Jesus Christ has saved me from sin."
He said, "I've been one of your critics." And so the man when he brought up there, he said, "Reverend Branham, it's always been said that that was psychology." Said, "I've heard of your meetings and said that it was psychology. Wasn't nothing to it. It was just imagination. When that light would come in, everybody would say, 'There it is. There it is,' and people would say well that's… Everybody just imagined it."
It was took once before by a newspaper, but it wasn't real. They wouldn't let it go because it wasn't by the American Photographer Association. It wasn't a member of it so they wouldn't pass it, but this was strictly the American Photographer Association.
text_no:
Jeg sa: "Min." Mange aviser og lignende lå rundt. De var interessert i å se hans rapport.
Han sa: "Stå opp." Jeg reiste meg. Han sa: "Du vil forlate dette livet en dag, som alle dødelige, Pastor Branham."
Jeg sa: "Det er jeg klar over, men takk Gud," sa jeg, "Jesus Kristus har frelst meg fra synd."
Han sa: "Jeg har vært en av dine kritikere." Og da mannen kom opp der, sa han: "Pastor Branham, det har alltid blitt sagt at det var psykologi." Han fortsatte: "Jeg har hørt om dine møter og det ble sagt at det bare var psykologi. Det var ingenting i det. Det var bare innbilning. Når det lyset kom inn, sa alle: 'Der er det. Der er det,' og folk sa det bare var innbilning."
Dette ble tatt en gang tidligere av en avis, men det ble ikke anerkjent. De lot det ikke gå videre fordi det ikke var av medlem av den amerikanske fotografiorganisasjonen. Men dette var strengt tatt fra den amerikanske fotografiorganisasjonen.

text:
And he said, "Reverend Branham, I have said it was psychology, but the mechanical eye of this camera will not take psychology." Said, "The Light struck the lens." Everybody begin crying.
They called me forward to present the picture to me and I said, "Well, it's not mine. It belongs to them."
He said, "Do you know that's worth a hundred thousand doll…"
I said, "Brother, do you mean to tell me just what he got through saying, that the first time in all the world's history that a supernatural Being was ever photographed. And here's what he said. Said, "The old hypocrite, (he meant the unbeliever) the old unbeliever, has always said that there was no scientific way to prove a supernatural Being." Said, "Now, they can't say that no more. Here's absolutely scientific proof that there is a supernatural Being," see. And scientifically proved by this mechanical camera here that took the light of that Angel standing there and said, "It absolutely is the truth."
text_no:
Han sa: "Pastor Branham, jeg har sagt at det var psykologi, men det mekaniske øyet til dette kameraet registrerer ikke psykologi." Han sa, "Lyset traff linsen." Alle begynte å gråte.
De kalte meg fram for å overrekke bildet til meg, og jeg sa, "Vel, det er ikke mitt. Det tilhører dem."
Han sa, "Vet du at dette er verdt hundre tusen dollar…"
Jeg sa, "Bror, mener du å fortelle meg det han nettopp sa, at for første gang i verdenshistorien har et overnaturlig Vesen blitt fotografert. Og her er hva han sa. Han sa, "Den gamle hykleren, (han mente den vantro) den gamle vantro, har alltid sagt at det ikke finnes noen vitenskapelig måte å bevise et overnaturlig Vesen på." Han sa, "Nå kan de ikke si det lenger. Her er absolutt vitenskapelig bevis på at det finnes et overnaturlig Vesen." Og vitenskapelig bevist av dette mekaniske kameraet som tok bildet av lyset fra den Engelen som sto der, og sa, "Det er absolutt sannheten."

text:
And I said, "Well, if Jesus Christ thought enough of me to come down and have His picture taken by me for the first time in all the world, you think I'm a little enough to commercialize His picture?"
And he said, "No, I wouldn't think that."
I said, "I turned it over to the Douglas Studios."
He said, "Well, there ought to be some kind of agreement."
I said, "Sure. They can sell it." And I said, "They can, as long as they keep it cheap enough that poor people can get it."
He said, "Some day, Reverend Branham, you may be dead and gone, but" said, "it'll be sold in ten-cent stores."
And I said, "Well, for the glory of God that would be fine."
text_no:
Jeg sa, "Vel, hvis Jesus Kristus verdsatte meg nok til å komme ned og la Seg avfotografere av meg, den første gangen i verdenshistorien, tror du da at jeg ville være så liten at jeg ville kommersialisere bildet av Ham?"
Han svarte, "Nei, det tenker jeg ikke."
Jeg sa, "Jeg overlot det til Douglas Studios."
Han sa, "Vel, det burde være en slags avtale."
Jeg svarte, "Selvsagt. De kan selge det. Så lenge prisen holdes lav nok til at fattige mennesker har råd til det."
Han sa, "En dag, pastor Branham, kan det hende du er død og borte, men det vil bli solgt i ti-kroners butikker."
Jeg sa, "For Guds ære ville det være helt fint."

text:
So then, tonight the picture's here. George J. Lacy is living today. He still in this work, and the picture lives on. If I would die this night, what I've testified about, the scientific world can't say it isn't so because there's scientific machinery and proof, that I've told the truth. And the church of millions of people that's been preached to in the past few years have seen it and watched it, and you know it yourself, that I've told the truth. And God testified that it is the truth. That's right. So the testimony is absolutely truth.
Now look, Christian friends. You're looking for something way out yonder to come. You're looking for something way back this way. Don't do that. It's right here now--right here now--just as much as you'll ever see. See? Only you might see it in greater quantity, but the Holy Spirit is here now which is the Lord Jesus Christ in His resurrection.
text_no:
Bildet er her i kveld. George J. Lacy lever fortsatt i dag og arbeider fremdeles, og bildet lever videre. Selv om jeg skulle dø i natt, kan ikke den vitenskapelige verden nekte for at jeg har fortalt sannheten, for det finnes vitenskapelige bevis og dokumentasjon som støtter dette. Menigheten, som består av millioner av mennesker som har lyttet til forkynnelsen de siste årene, har sett det og erfart det, og dere vet selv at jeg har fortalt sannheten. Og Gud har vitnet om at det er sannheten. Det er riktig. Så vitnesbyrdet er absolutt sant.
Se nå her, kristne venner. Dere leter etter noe som skal komme langt der borte, eller noe som har vært langt bak i tid. Ikke gjør det. Det er her og nå – rett her og nå – like mye som dere noen gang vil se. Dere kan kanskje se det i større mengde, men Den Hellige Ånd er her nå, som er Herren Jesus Kristus i Hans oppstandelse.

text:
It's so marvelous. I remember something else happened there when Brother Bosworth come into the room the next day. And he had the picture of Florence Nightingale. How many ever heard of Florence Nightingale? Sure. She founded the Red Cross.
Her granddaughter from London, England, she was under the British Crown and at Durban, South Africa dying, which she didn't know about, with cancer on the … duodenal cancer in her bowels. They showed me her picture, and Brother Bosworth, I, and my wife, all wept when we seen it. She was next to the smallest woman I ever seen in my life. Eat up. Her limbs up here around the thigh--wasn't over about that big around.
text_no:
Det er så fantastisk. Jeg husker at noe annet skjedde der da Bror Bosworth kom inn i rommet dagen etter. Han hadde med seg et bilde av Florence Nightingale. Hvor mange har hørt om Florence Nightingale? Klart. Hun grunnla Røde Kors.
Hennes barnebarn fra London, England, og under den britiske kronen, lå for døden i Durban, Sør-Afrika, uten å vite om det, med duodenalkreft i tarmene. De viste meg bildet hennes, og Bror Bosworth, min kone og jeg gråt alle da vi så det. Hun var en av de minste kvinnene jeg noen gang har sett i mitt liv. Fullstendig oppspist. Hennes lår var ikke større enn dette rundt.

text: Now, Georgie Carter of Middletown, Indiana which had been laying on her back nine years and eight months was healed of TB by a vision. I didn't even know where the place was and went down there, and there she was just as He said. She's my piano player in the Milltown Baptist Church where I'm still pastor down there--had an associate tonight. She's a piano player there and been on her bed for nine years and eight months, and never moved from the bed. But she only weighed thirty-five pounds approximately, but TB had eat her up. Now that's not my statement. That's doctors' statements and any of these things are absolutely doctors' statements. If they cannot back it up with a doctor they have to be proven and not by ministers, not by… They have to be proven by doctors before we can put them because they have to be that way. And now she was healed.
text_no: Georgie Carter fra Middletown, Indiana, hadde ligget på ryggen i ni år og åtte måneder og ble helbredet for tuberkulose gjennom et syn. Jeg visste ikke engang hvor stedet var, men dro dit og fant henne akkurat som Han hadde sagt. Hun er pianist i Milltown Baptist Menighet, der jeg fortsatt er pastor—hadde en assistent i kveld. Hun hadde ligget i sengen i ni år og åtte måneder og ikke beveget seg fra sengen. Hun veide bare omtrent femten kilo, da tuberkulosen hadde tæret på henne. Dette er ikke mine uttalelser, men legenes vurderinger, og alle slike ting er absolutt legenes uttalelser. Hvis de ikke kan underbygges av leger, må de bevises og ikke av predikanter. De må bevises av leger før vi kan publisere dem, fordi de må være slik. Og nå er hun helbredet.

text:
And Florence Nightingale, a very lovely woman, they wanted to send… They had two airplane tickets that had already come in for me to come to Durban, Africa. I laid it on the floor and Brother Bosworth and I prayed. I said, "Lord, if You will heal this woman, I will go to Africa. I can't go now. Looky here." So I just committed it to the Lord, sent a handkerchief back to her and went on.
Not long after that, King George of England … when a man by the name of, I forget, at Fort Wayne. You remember the meeting in Fort Wayne Gospel Tabernacle, was a businessman of the city. He had multiple sclerosis and had been a bed patient ten years. And a vision come over the man, and he was made perfectly whole. Walked… Leinman was his name. Walked up.
text_no:
Florence Nightingale, en meget vakker kvinne, ønsket å sende meg til Durban, Afrika. Jeg hadde allerede mottatt to flybilletter. Bror Bosworth og jeg la dem på gulvet og ba sammen. Jeg sa: "Herre, hvis Du vil helbrede denne kvinnen, vil jeg dra til Afrika. Jeg kan ikke dra nå. Se her." Jeg overlot det til Herren, sendte et lommetørkle tilbake til henne, og fortsatte videre.
Ikke lenge etter skjedde noe med Kong George av England. En mann fra Fort Wayne, navnet hans unnslipper meg, men du husker møtet i Fort Wayne Gospel Tabernacle. Han var forretningsmann i byen og hadde hatt multippel sklerose og vært sengeliggende i ti år. En visjon kom over ham, og han ble helt helbredet. Han, Leinman var navnet hans, kunne gå.

text:
And he was a friend to the king's private secretary, and through there, King George of England sent word to me. I have his statements and have his letters with his seals, to come pray for him with multiple sclerosis. And so, I couldn't go up that time. So I just wired back and told the king that I would pray for him here. That God would heal here just the same as He would over there. And so, then another telegram come through and wanted me to come on over immediately.
Later when I went to England, over there, to see him the Lord healed him. He couldn't even stand up over five minutes at a time, and I believe the second day, he played eighteen holes of golf. And never was bothered with it no more until the very day he died. And I was in Africa when he died. They found a little tumor here on his lung. They started to cut it open and air got to it some way, I don't know, and caused a blood clot to go to his brain and killed him instantly. So, a very fine man.
text_no:
Han var venn med kongens private sekretær, og gjennom ham sendte kong George av England en forespørsel til meg. Jeg har både hans uttalelser og brevene med seglene som ba meg om å komme og be for ham, da han led av multippel sklerose. Jeg kunne ikke reise da, så jeg svarte telegrafisk og informerte kongen om at jeg ville be for ham herfra, og at Gud kunne helbrede her like godt som der. En ny telegram kom, og jeg ble bedt om umiddelbart å reise over.
Senere, da jeg reiste til England for å se ham, helbredet Herren ham. Han kunne ikke stå oppreist i mer enn fem minutter om gangen, men jeg tror det var på den andre dagen at han spilte atten hull golf. Han hadde ingen plager med sykdommen igjen før den dagen han døde. Jeg var i Afrika da han døde. Han hadde fått en liten svulst på lungen. Da de begynte å operere den, kom luft på en eller annen måte til svulsten, som forårsaket en blodpropp som gikk til hjernen og drepte ham umiddelbart. Han var en svært fin mann.

text:
But when we landed in England at the international airport, thousands of people, of course, had packed in. And they kept paging him there. And Brother Baxter, one of the managers, went in to find out what they wanted. And it was a man said, "The plane … Florence Nightingale is laying in a plane dying."
How she knowed I was there, I don't know, but she had come from Africa. Flown in there and was laying, dying out in the next plane. And so the minister wanted to know if I could go to her. Why, they was just packed and jammed in there. Certainly not. Why, I couldn't have got through there if I had to, and I said, "No, I couldn't go right now." I said, "You take her to your house, and I'm going over to Piccadilly Hotel, then down to Buckingham Palace, out to Westminster Abbey, then I will be right back at Piccadilly Hotel and you take her to your parsonage." It was an Anglican minister and I said, "Then I will go over and pray with her."
He said, "Brother Branham, she won't live."
I said, "Well, I can't get to her there, sir. You know what it would be if I get out of this line here--what it would be.
He said, "Well, all right. I will try it." So he said, "They think she's dying. They can't see any life in her now."
text_no:
Da vi landet på den internasjonale flyplassen i England, var det tusenvis av mennesker som hadde samlet seg. De ropte stadig opp navnet hans. Bror Baxter, en av lederne, gikk inn for å finne ut hva de ville. Det viste seg å være en mann som sa: "Flyet ... Florence Nightingale ligger i et fly og dør."
Hvordan hun visste at jeg var der, vet jeg ikke, men hun hadde kommet fra Afrika, fløyet dit og lå døende i et fly like ved. Forkynneren ville vite om jeg kunne gå til henne. Det var helt umulig, folk var pakket og stappet sammen. Jeg kunne ikke komme meg gjennom, så jeg sa: "Nei, jeg kan ikke gå akkurat nå. Ta henne til hjemmet ditt, og jeg skal til Piccadilly Hotel, deretter Buckingham Palace, ut til Westminster Abbey, og så tilbake til Piccadilly Hotel. Ta henne til presteboligen din."
Det var en anglikansk prest, og jeg sa: "Så går jeg over og ber for henne."
Han sa: "Bror Branham, hun vil ikke leve."
Jeg sa: "Vel, jeg kan ikke nå henne der, sir. Du vet hvordan det ville bli hvis jeg prøvde å forlate denne linjen."
Han sa: "Vel, greit. Jeg skal prøve. De tror hun er døende. De kan ikke se noe liv i henne nå."

text:
And so I said, "Well, you take her on then and we will see because I can't get there." But I went on down. The king wasn't in at the time. I come back over to Westminster Abbey, and a great group of ministers, you know, and all that night they never got me back to the hotel till about, oh, two o'clock in the morning.
Then the next morning early… Is anybody here ever in England? It's around in April. It's so foggy. Oh, my. You can hardly see your hand before you. Little bitty car drove up and they come and got me--went down to this minister's to the rectory. And when I went upstairs, some of the managers, Mr. Gordon Lindsay, you know the writer of the Voice of Healing, Mr. Baxter and all, many managers, we went up to the room. And when I went in there, there sat her doctor, two nurses and two or three ministers.
text_no:
Jeg sa, "Vel, ta dere av henne, så får vi se, for jeg kan ikke komme dit." Men jeg dro videre. Kongen var ikke til stede. Jeg kom tilbake til Westminster Abbey, der en stor gruppe forkynnere var samlet. Hele natten holdt de meg opptatt, og jeg var ikke tilbake på hotellet før rundt klokken to om morgenen.
Neste morgen tidlig... Er det noen her som har vært i England i april? Det er så tåkete at du knapt kan se hånden foran deg. En liten bil hentet meg og kjørte meg til en forkynners bolig. Da jeg kom opp på rommet, var noen av lederne allerede der, blant annet Gordon Lindsay, forfatter av Voice of Healing, og mange andre ledere. Når jeg gikk inn, satt der en lege, to sykepleiere og to eller tre forkynnere.

text:
We entered the room. And we had a very fine welcome by the people. And so I walked over to see Mrs. Nightingale. Now, if she hadn't eat a bite that would have been, I guess, two months before that or more. Hadn't it, Mr. Bosworth? Now, she hadn't eaten a bite since then, and her limbs then were just about like that. And her legs were all blue where the veins had collapsed. And it was a terrible looking sight.
So, I looked at her and I seen her face--how thin and all eaten away. And I said … her doctor there. I said, "Any chance for her?"
He said, "Not a chance."
I said, "Oh, my! It's too bad." I walked over. I said, "How do you do, Mrs. Nightingale?"
She couldn't speak. She was moving her lips, but she didn't have strength enough till I could hear her. The nurse got down, and she said, "She wants to shake your hand, Brother Branham."--the nurse said.
